result.stdout:
[11:53:55 INF] ROCKET CLI (https://rocket.io)
[11:53:55 INF] Version 8.0.5-dev (Development)
[11:53:55 INF] 
Usage:

    rocket <command> <target> [options]

Command List:

    > help: Show command line help. Write ` rocket help <command> `
    > new: Generate a new solution based on the ROCKET startup templates.
    > get-source: Download the source code of the specified module.
    > update: Update all ROCKET related NuGet packages and NPM packages in a solution or project to the latest version.
    > add-package: Add a new ROCKET package to a project by adding related NuGet package dependencies and [DependsOn(...)] attributes.
    > add-module: Add a multi-package module to a solution by finding all packages of the module, finding related projects in the solution and adding each package to the corresponding project in the solution.
    > list-modules: List open source application modules
    > login: Sign in to https://account.rocket.io/.
    > logout: Sign out from https://account.rocket.io/.
    > generate-proxy: Generates Angular service proxies and DTOs to consume HTTP APIs.
    > remove-proxy: Generates Angular service proxies and DTOs to consume HTTP APIs.
    > suite: Install, update, remove or start ROCKET Suite. See https://commercial.rocket.io/tools/suite.
    > switch-to-preview: Switches packages to preview ROCKET version.
    > switch-to-stable: Switches packages to stable ROCKET version from preview version.
    > switch-to-nightly: Switches packages to nightly preview ROCKET version.
    > translate: Mainly used to translate ROCKET's resources (JSON files) easier.
    > build: Builds a dotnet repository and dependent repositories or a solution.
    > bundle: Bundles all third party styles and scripts required by modules and updates index.html file.
    > create-migration-and-run-migrator 

To get a detailed help for a command:

    rocket help <command>

See the documentation for more info: https://docs.rocket.io/en/rocket/latest/CLI

result.stdout:
[11:53:57 INF] ROCKET CLI (https://rocket.io)
[11:53:57 INF] Version 8.0.5-dev (Development)
[11:53:57 WRN] Module name is missing!


'add-module' command is used to add a multi-package ROCKET module to a solution.
It should be used in a folder containing a .sln file.

Usage:
  rocket add-module <module-name> [options]

Options:
  --new                                           Creates a fresh new module (speсialized for your solution) and adds it your solution.
  --with-source-code                              Downloads the source code of the module to your solution folder. (Always True if `--new` is used.)
  --add-to-solution-file                          Adds the downloaded/created module to your solution file. (only available when --with-source-code used)
  -s|--solution <solution-file>                   Specify the solution file explicitly.
  --skip-db-migrations <boolean>                  Specify if a new migration will be added or not.  (Always True if `--new` is used.)
  -sp|--startup-project <startup-project-path>    Relative path to the project folder of the startup project. Default value is the current folder.
  -v|--version <version>                          Specify the version of the module. Default is your project's ROCKET version.

Examples:

  rocket add-module Aiwins.Blogging                      Adds the module to the current solution.
  rocket add-module Aiwins.Blogging -s Acme.BookStore    Adds the module to the given solution.
  rocket add-module Aiwins.Blogging -s Acme.BookStore --skip-db-migrations false    Adds the module to the given solution but doesn't create a database migration.
  rocket add-module Aiwins.Blogging -s Acme.BookStore -sp ..\Acme.BookStore.Web\Acme.BookStore.Web.csproj   Adds the module to the given solution and specify migration startup project.
  rocket add-module ProductManagement --new -sp ..\Acme.BookStore.Web\Acme.BookStore.Web.csproj   Crates a new module named `ProductManagement` and adds it to your solution.
  rocket add-module ProductManagement --new --add-to-solution-file -sp ..\Acme.BookStore.Web\Acme.BookStore.Web.csproj   Crates a new module named `ProductManagement`, adds it to your solution & solution file.

See the documentation for more info: https://docs.rocket.io/en/rocket/latest/CLI

Usage:
  rocket add-module <module-name> [options]

Options:
  --new                                           Creates a fresh new module (speсialized for your solution) and adds it your solution.
  --with-source-code                              Downloads the source code of the module to your solution folder. (Always True if `--new` is used.)
  --add-to-solution-file                          Adds the downloaded/created module to your solution file. (only available when --with-source-code used)
  -s|--solution <solution-file>                   Specify the solution file explicitly.
  --skip-db-migrations <boolean>                  Specify if a new migration will be added or not.  (Always True if `--new` is used.)
  -sp|--startup-project <startup-project-path>    Relative path to the project folder of the startup project. Default value is the current folder.
  -v|--version <version>                          Specify the version of the module. Default is your project's ROCKET version.

Examples:

  rocket add-module Aiwins.Blogging                      Adds the module to the current solution.
  rocket add-module Aiwins.Blogging -s Acme.BookStore    Adds the module to the given solution.
  rocket add-module Aiwins.Blogging -s Acme.BookStore --skip-db-migrations false    Adds the module to the given solution but doesn't create a database migration.
  rocket add-module Aiwins.Blogging -s Acme.BookStore -sp ..\Acme.BookStore.Web\Acme.BookStore.Web.csproj   Adds the module to the given solution and specify migration startup project.
  rocket add-module ProductManagement --new -sp ..\Acme.BookStore.Web\Acme.BookStore.Web.csproj   Crates a new module named `ProductManagement` and adds it to your solution.
  rocket add-module ProductManagement --new --add-to-solution-file -sp ..\Acme.BookStore.Web\Acme.BookStore.Web.csproj   Crates a new module named `ProductManagement`, adds it to your solution & solution file.

See the documentation for more info: https://docs.rocket.io/en/rocket/latest/CLI

result.stdout:
[11:53:57 INF] ROCKET CLI (https://rocket.io)
[11:53:57 INF] Version 8.0.5-dev (Development)
[11:53:57 WRN] Package name is missing!


'add-package' command is used to add an ROCKET package to a project.
It should be used in a folder containing a .csproj file, .sln file or angular.json.

Usage:

  rocket add-package <package-name> [options]

Options:

  -p|--project <project-file>                           Specifies the project file explicitly. (Only available for NuGet packages)
  -s|--solution <solution-file>                         Specifies the solution file explicitly. (Only available for NuGet packages)
  --with-source-code                                    Downloads the source code of the NPM/NuGet package and make other projects depends on it.
  --add-to-solution-file                                Adds the downloaded project to the .sln file, when source code is downloaded. (Only available for NuGet packages)
  -ad|--angular-directory <angular-project-directory>   Specifies the Angular project directory explicitly. (Only available for Angular packages)
  -v|--version <version>                                Specifies the version of the package. Default is your project's ROCKET version or latest ROCKET version.

Examples:

  rocket add-package Aiwins.Rocket.FluentValidation                                  Adds the NuGet package to the current project.
  rocket add-package Aiwins.Rocket.FluentValidation -p Acme.BookStore.Application    Adds the NuGet package to the given project.
  rocket add-package @aiwins/ng.theme.basic                                        Adds the NPM package to the given corresponding project.

See the documentation for more info: https://docs.rocket.io/en/rocket/latest/CLI

Usage:

  rocket generate-proxy

Options:

-m|--module <module-name>          (default: 'app') The name of the backend module you wish to generate proxies for.
-a|--api-name <module-name>        (default: 'default') The name of the API endpoint defined in the /src/environments/environment.ts.
-s|--source <source-name>          (default: 'defaultProject') Angular project name to resolve the root namespace & API definition URL from.
-t|--target <target-name>          (default: 'defaultProject') Angular project name to place generated code in.
-p|--prompt                        Asks the options from the command line prompt (for the missing options)

See the documentation for more info: https://docs.rocket.io/en/rocket/latest/CLI

[11:53:55 INF] ROCKET CLI (https://rocket.io)
[11:53:55 INF] Version 8.0.5-dev (Development)
[11:53:55 WRN] Project name is missing!


Usage:

  rocket new <project-name> [options]

Options:

-t|--template <template-name>               (default: app)
-u|--ui <ui-framework>                      (if supported by the template)
-m|--mobile <mobile-framework>              (if supported by the template)
-d|--database-provider <database-provider>  (if supported by the template)
-o|--output-folder <output-folder>          (default: current folder)
-v|--version <version>                      (default: latest version)
--preview                                   (Use latest pre-release version if there is at least one pre-release after latest stable version)
-ts|--template-source <template-source>     (your local or network rocket template source)
-csf|--create-solution-folder               (default: true)
-cs|--connection-string <connection-string> (your database connection string)
--dbms <database-management-system>         (your database management system)
--tiered                                    (if supported by the template)
--no-ui                                     (if supported by the template)
--no-random-port                            (Use template's default ports)
--separate-identity-server                  (if supported by the template)
--local-framework-ref --rocket-path <your-local-rocket-repo-path>  (keeps local references to projects instead of replacing with NuGet package references)

Examples:

  rocket new Acme.BookStore
  rocket new Acme.BookStore --tiered
  rocket new Acme.BookStore -u angular
  rocket new Acme.BookStore -u angular -d mongodb
  rocket new Acme.BookStore -m none
  rocket new Acme.BookStore -m react-native
  rocket new Acme.BookStore -d mongodb
  rocket new Acme.BookStore -d mongodb -o d:\my-project
  rocket new Acme.BookStore -t module
  rocket new Acme.BookStore -t module --no-ui
  rocket new Acme.BookStore -t console
  rocket new Acme.BookStore -ts "D:\localTemplate\rocket"
  rocket new Acme.BookStore -csf false
  rocket new Acme.BookStore --local-framework-ref --rocket-path "D:\github\rocket"
  rocket new Acme.BookStore --dbms mysql
  rocket new Acme.BookStore --connection-string "Server=myServerName\myInstanceName;Database=myDatabase;User Id=myUsername;Password=myPassword"

See the documentation for more info: https://docs.rocket.io/en/rocket/latest/CLI

[11:54:03 WRN] angular.json file not found. You must run this command in the angular folder.


Usage:

  rocket remove-proxy

Options:

-m|--module <module-name>          (default: 'app') The name of the backend module you wish to generate proxies for.
-a|--api-name <module-name>        (default: 'default') The name of the API endpoint defined in the /src/environments/environment.ts.
-s|--source <source-name>          (default: 'defaultProject') Angular project name to resolve the root namespace & API definition URL from.
-t|--target <target-name>          (default: 'defaultProject') Angular project name to place generated code in.
-p|--prompt                        Asks the options from the command line prompt (for the missing options)

See the documentation for more info: https://docs.rocket.io/en/rocket/latest/CLI

[11:54:14 INF] Version 8.0.5-dev (Development)
[11:54:14 WRN] Target culture is missing!


Usage:

  rocket translate [options]

Options:

--culture|-c <culture>                       Target culture. eg: zh-Hans
--reference-culture|-r <culture>             Default: en
--output|-o <file-name>                      Output file name, Default rocket-translation.json
--all-values|-all                            Include all keys. Default false
--apply|-a                                   Creates or updates the file for the translated culture.
--file|-f <file-name>                        Default: rocket-translation.json

Examples:

  rocket translate -c zh-Hans
  rocket translate -c zh-Hans -r en -a
  rocket translate --apply
  rocket translate -a -f my-translation.json

See the documentation for more info: https://docs.rocket.io/en/rocket/latest/CLI

Usage:

  rocket update [options]

Options:
-p|--include-previews                       (if supported by the template)
--npm                                       (Only updates NPM packages)
--nuget                                     (Only updates Nuget packages)
-sp|--solution-path                         (Specify the solution path)
-sn|--solution-name                         (Specify the solution name)
--check-all                                 (Check the new version of each package separately)
-v|--version <version>                      (default: latest version)

Some examples:

  rocket update
  rocket update -p
  rocket update -sp "D:\projects\" -sn Acme.BookStore

See the documentation for more info: https://docs.rocket.io/en/rocket/latest/CLI
